comment: Community-extensible, locale-aware catalog of job-posting red-flag signals (REQ-8002, REQ-8005). Data, not logic: adding a signal is a data edit. `globalSignals` apply everywhere; `byRegion` adds market-specific scams keyed by locale-pack code. The drafter weighs cited evidence into a Legitimacy Verdict (see business-rules §10 and 08-legitimacy.md). Never accuse without cited evidence (ARCH-0007); the system warns, the user decides (ARCH-0006).

severityLevels.high: A strong scam indicator on its own — one is usually enough for a Suspicious verdict.
severityLevels.medium: Concerning; two or more push toward Caution/Suspicious.

globalSignals.label: Upfront fee or payment
globalSignals.description: Asks the candidate to pay for training, equipment, background checks, a 'starter kit', or a visa/processing fee.
examples: purchase your own equipment up front and we reimburse later

globalSignals.label: Personal-data / ID / banking harvesting
globalSignals.description: Requests sensitive data (national ID, passport scan, bank account, SSN, copies of cards) before any genuine interview or offer.
examples: send a copy of your ID and bank details to set up payroll before interview

globalSignals.label: Off-platform redirect
globalSignals.description: Pushes the conversation immediately to personal messaging (Telegram/WhatsApp/Signal) or a personal email, away from any verifiable channel.
examples: contact our hiring manager directly on Telegram @...

globalSignals.label: Too-good-to-be-true compensation
globalSignals.description: Pay far above market for little experience or vague duties; 'earn $X/day from home, no experience'.

globalSignals.label: Vague or absent company identity
globalSignals.description: No verifiable company name, domain, registration, or web presence; generic free-email contact; mismatched company/domain.
examples: recruiter emails from a gmail address with no company website

globalSignals.label: High-pressure / urgency tactics
globalSignals.description: Artificial urgency, 'offer expires today', pressure to accept or pay before verifying.
examples: you must confirm and pay the deposit within 2 hours

globalSignals.label: Unverifiable contact details
globalSignals.description: Contact name/title/phone cannot be corroborated against the stated company.
examples: hiring manager name does not appear anywhere at the company

globalSignals.label: Offer without process
globalSignals.description: A job 'offer' or hire with no real interview or assessment.
examples: you're hired! just complete onboarding payment

globalSignals.label: Overpayment / money-movement
globalSignals.description: Asks the candidate to receive and forward money, deposit a check and send part back, or process payments — a money-mule pattern.
examples: we'll send a check; buy equipment and wire the remainder to our vendor

byRegion.comment: Region keys match locale-pack codes (REQ-8005). Communities can add market-specific scams here without touching core.

IN.label: Placement/registration fee
IN.description: Consultancy demands a registration or placement fee to forward your profile.
